2606.27826.title: NormAct: A Benchmark for Hidden Social Norm Compliance in Embodied Planning
2606.27826.paper_summary: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are increasingly deployed as embodied planners in egocentric environments, where task success requires not only achieving instructed goals but also acting in socially appropriate ways. While explicit goals may render certain actions optimal, implicit social norms often impose hidden constraints. Existing evaluations typically focus on explicit goal achievement or direct norm knowledge, seldom assessing whether planners can infer and apply these hidden constraints within action sequences. We introduce NormAct, a benchmark for embodied social-norm interactions that evaluates plans on Goal Achievement, Norm Compliance, and overall Task Success. NormAct uniquely embeds hidden norms within ordinary tasks, testing whether models can realize them without explicit instruction. Experiments with state-of-the-art MLLMs (GPT-5.4, Claude Opus 4.7, Gemini 3 Pro) reveal a significant gap: models achieve explicit goals in 67.3\% of cases, but comply with hidden norms in only 26.4\%. Cue-condition experiments indicate that this gap stems not from a lack of general social knowledge, but from challenges in activating and grounding relevant norms in context. To address this, we propose NormPerceptor, a context-conditioned cue generator that infers scene-relevant norms prior to planning, increasing Task Success from 24.2\% to 46.7\%. Our results underscore the importance of enabling embodied agents to proactively detect hidden norms, ground them in visual evidence, and integrate them as action-planning constraints. Our benchmark is publicly available at https://huggingface.co/datasets/Caleb196x/NormAct.
2606.27826.paper_summary_zh: 多模態大型語言模型（MLLMs）越來越多地被用作在自我中心環境中的具身規劃者，這裡的任務成功不僅需要實現指示的目標，還需要以社會適當的方式行動。雖然明確的目標可能使某些行動變得最佳，但隱含的社會規範往往施加隱藏的約束。現有的評估通常專注於明確目標的實現或直接的規範知識，鮮少評估規劃者是否能夠推斷並應用這些隱藏的約束於行動序列中。我們引入了NormAct，一個用於具身社會規範互動的基準，評估計劃在目標實現、規範遵循和整體任務成功方面的表現。NormAct獨特地將隱藏的規範嵌入普通任務中，測試模型是否能在沒有明確指示的情況下實現它們。對最先進的MLLMs（GPT-5.4、Claude Opus 4.7、Gemini 3 Pro）的實驗顯示出顯著的差距：模型在67.3\%的情況下實現明確目標，但僅在26.4\%的情況下遵守隱藏的規範。提示條件實驗表明，這一差距並非源於缺乏一般社會知識，而是來自於在上下文中激活和根植相關規範的挑戰。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了NormPerceptor，一個上下文條件的提示生成器，能在規劃之前推斷場景相關的規範，將任務成功率從24.2\%提高到46.7\%。我們的結果強調了使具身代理主動檢測隱藏規範、將其根植於視覺證據中並整合為行動規劃約束的重要性。我們的基準公開可用於 https://huggingface.co/datasets/Caleb196x/NormAct。
2606.27826.author: Shiyun Zhao et.al.
2606.27826.authors: Shiyun Zhao, Xinwei Song, Tianyu Guo, Xiaomeng Gao, Mingyuan Liu, Xu Han, Yuanyuan Zhang, Zhenliang Zhang, Xue Feng, Bo Dai
